source: /audit full — 2026-06-20 run-2 (post-remediation re-audit; score 88). Stacks on PR #48.
note: Residual + new findings after the run-1 remediation, incl. gaps r1 left (incomplete heal/snapshot lock, r1-introduced tautological test, hooks.json PostToolUse MultiEdit gap). Force-fix mode: every finding gets a code change (architect orders, does not document-only). lane ∈ {A,B,C,D}. The Resolve phase confirms/corrects suggestedEntityId against the vendored glossary.

lanes.A: wiki-dev-eng-retrieval — src/cli, src/commands/search, src/commands/verify (engine concurrency runner + parity tests), src/core/{report,fs,graph,stem,link-resolver,duplicate-claims}.ts
lanes.B: wiki-dev-eng-schema — scripts/resolve-vault.sh, src/core/{vault,firewall,ontology-profile}.ts + tests, src/commands/verify/check-entity-type.ts, src/data/{config,templates}.ts, schemas/

findings.summary: Unquoted heredoc delimiter (`<<FRONTMATTER`) expands `$(...)`/`${}` from LLM-authored session-scratch content → command injection. Fix: quote the delimiter `<<'FRONTMATTER'`.
Caller-supplied SESSION_ID used unvalidated in a `find -name` glob and the output path → glob injection + path escape. Fix: validate against `^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$` before any filesystem use.
PostToolUse matcher is `Write|Edit`; a MultiEdit write skips post-wiki-write.sh + post-ingest-summary.sh (the PreToolUse gates already cover MultiEdit). Add `MultiEdit` to the PostToolUse matcher.

The unified vault lock covers only the terminal append→commit; the fix/verify git ops run outside it. Wrap the FULL pre→fix→verify→commit sequence in withVaultLock/withVaultLockSync, matching the bash twin (snapshot.sh acquires the lock before any git op).

The `pre` checkpoint's ensureRepo + applyCheckpointMode git ops run outside the lock — asymmetric to scripts/snapshot.sh:89 ('acquire lock BEFORE any git op'). Acquire the lock before the pre-phase git ops.

resolveConcurrency + sortFindings + the Promise.all dispatch copied verbatim across verify and lint; CONCURRENCY_MIN/MAX re-declared in both. Extract one shared runChecks helper (cemented by pattern P-TMPL template-method).
score_candidate() ~71 lines with eval-threaded data flow. Decompose into focused functions (keep ADR-0017 score byte-identical — it gates local-model unlock).
Only untested production module; template formatting never asserted. Add a colocated unit suite (TDD).
Vault-local malformed entity_type_extensions fail-open branch under-covered. Add negative tests for the fail-open path.
13 command handlers build JSON output inline instead of routing through the existing emit() helper at :475. Route them through emit().
_fm_field() identical across the two scripts. Extract into a shared sourced helper (watch gate-05 verify-parity).
otherVaults split/filter block duplicated. DRY into one helper.
_resolve_source_wikilink 3-tier nested loops. Flatten with early returns/helpers (watch gate-05 verify-parity).

Hand-rolled Porter stemmer (documented zero-dep tradeoff). Force-fix: architect to scope a minimal, parity-safe change (a zero-dep stemmer is a legitimate NO-RAG/offline choice — do not add a network/runtime dep).
$endpoint interpolated into curl with no URL validation. Validate/whitelist the endpoint scheme+host before use.
$last refspec read from settings.json passed to git; git arg-parsing constrains exploitability but validate the value (12-hex commit or empty).
Degraded _settings_get_field fallback (no Bun) is not fail-closed → silent wrong-vault resolution. Fail closed (or warn loudly) when the field cannot be read reliably.

Broad Bash tool grant on the orchestrator agent; soft prompt-injection defense only (acknowledged in SECURITY.md). Force-fix: narrow the grant to the specific command families the orchestrator actually needs.
`.toBeTruthy()` on hardcoded-nonempty fields — introduced by the run-1 remediation. Replace with assertions that pin the parsed values.
Hardcoded error/warn counts as a parity baseline make the test brittle to unrelated check additions. Assert structurally (relative invariants), not exact magic counts.
No test for double-release / release-without-acquire. Add the negative case.
No test for an invalid gitCheckpoint mode. Add the negative case.
The bash error-exit parity path is untested. Add a case that asserts engine↔bash agree on a failing vault.
FIFO-order test relies on scheduler timing. Make ordering deterministic (await explicit sequencing, not timing).
Wall-clock + fake-sleep with a 1s timeout. Make it deterministic (inject the clock / drop the real sleep).
vault + vaultName raw-string pair threaded together. Introduce a small vault-identity value object (reuse the link-resolver branded-type approach where it fits).
vault-identity pair + raw filePath. Same value-object treatment as R26.
"0.9700" unnamed cap. Name a constant.
ollama_chat_call presence guard triplicated. Extract a shared sourced helper.
canonicalForm exported with no external consumer. Remove the export (or inline) if truly unused.
checkChangedWikilinks is test-only. Remove or wire it to a real consumer.
provider: "ollama"|"lmstudio" enum in the domain model. Force-fix: architect to scope (the enum is a small, deliberate local-model surface — keep it a typed boundary, do not over-abstract).
No back-off between fix/verify iterations (bounded + progress-guarded, marginal). Add a small back-off or document the bound.

patterns.summary: Extract a shared runChecks(...) skeleton (resolveConcurrency + sortFindings + Promise.all dispatch) consumed by verify and lint — cements the R06 golden-hammer fix structurally.
Formalize the anonymous CheckFn[] (verify.ts:101, doctor.ts:391) into a keyed check registry, mirroring the hook GATE_REGISTRY Strategy already present.
Compose the 13 *-check.ts rule modules as composable Specification<Vault> objects (and/or/not). Architect to scope — large; do incrementally.
Introduce a VaultPageRepository port over the vault-page filesystem access scattered across verify/fix/heal/propose/migrate. Architect to scope — large.
Split src/core into domain/ (pure) + infra/ (git/fs/log). Architect-led, largest blast radius — scope a narrow first slice, do not big-bang.
